## [03:04 - 06:06]

Direct marketing because I'm super good at it But I've often found that when I go indirect it has better results for my audience and so what I would advise you is Take some time to create both versions Even if you're just doing a minimum viable funnel and you're going straight to the sales page and your copy is short Take some time to create a direct version of that and an indirect version of that So if we're talking about that skateboard brand again, right and the surfing persona the direct version of that was custom skateboards for surfers at heart The indirect version of that could be The waves Hitting the waves on the pavement what it will feel like When you hit those waves on the pavement for example, right? So you're just being you're not being so direct about what you're saying You're not talking directly to that product or solution or problem So I would strongly advise that you create two variations of that for your copy and When you're creating copy by the way for your clients or for your own campaign Once you have this kind of set up here that we were working from before you can pretty much stitch everything together in hundreds of different variations So if you think about it, this is just an example, right?
